question_text: What is the role of an HR manager?
ideal_answer_summary: An HR manager oversees recruitment employee relations policy implementation and ensures legal compliance.
Recruitment is the process of identifying attracting and hiring suitable candidates.
Onboarding introduces new employees to company culture policies and job responsibilities.
Employee engagement refers to employees commitment and involvement in their work.
Performance appraisal is the formal assessment of an employees performance.
HR compliance ensures company follows labor laws and regulations.
Payroll management handles salary processing tax deductions and benefits.
Talent management focuses on developing and retaining employees.
Employee retention strategies reduce turnover and improve satisfaction.
Diversity promotes equal opportunities and inclusive workplace culture.
A job description outlines duties responsibilities and qualifications for a role.
Training and development improves employee skills and career growth.
Compensation includes salary bonuses and benefits offered to employees.
Employee grievance refers to workplace complaints or disputes.
HR policies define workplace rules and procedures.
Succession planning prepares employees for future leadership roles.
Exit interviews collect feedback from departing employees.
Organizational culture represents shared values and work environment.
Probation period is a trial phase to assess employee suitability.
Workforce planning forecasts staffing needs to meet business goals.
Job analysis identifies tasks skills and requirements of a position.
HRIS is a system for managing employee data and HR processes.
Absenteeism refers to frequent absence from work.
Employee turnover measures the rate of employees leaving the company.
Employer branding promotes company reputation to attract talent.
Workplace ethics refer to moral principles guiding employee behavior.
